title: Windows 11 is getting support for 1,000 Hz+ monitors soon as part of Insider builds Microsoft has reportedly increased the refresh rate limit to 5,000 Hz
summary: Windows 11 Insider builds now support 1,000 Hz+ monitors, with a limit of 5,000 Hz. This feature is part of the Release Preview channel, indicating a near-final release. Nvidia has also updated its G-Sync Pulsar displays.
insight: Higher refresh rates enhance gaming and video performance significantly always.

title: Tech titans team up to form optical interconnect alliance to solve the AI buildout's big data bottleneck Nvidia, AMD, Broadcom & more set sights on building PHY to break through the limitations of copper
summary: Tech giants form alliance to develop a standardized optical interconnect, aiming to break the copper speed limit with a PHY capable of up to 3.2 Tb/s.
insight: Breaking copper speed limitations enables faster AI data processing.

title: Nvidia claims 1 million times better path tracing performance is coming in future gaming GPUs says current GPUs are already 10,000x faster than Pascal
summary: Nvidia claims its future gaming GPUs will offer 1 million times better path tracing performance, a significant improvement over the current-gen Blackwell family, which is already 100,000 times better than Pascal.
insight: Enhanced path tracing boosts realism in gaming graphics and immersion

title: The ongoing Strait of Hormuz blockage will impact the semiconductor and AI industries with Aluminum, Helium and LNG shortages and with no timeline for re-opening, supply chains face significant challenges
summary: The Strait of Hormuz blockage threatens chip and AI industries with shortages, including aluminum, helium, and LNG, impacting global supply chains with no clear timeline for re-opening.
insight: Global supply chains face significant disruptions due to Strait of Hormuz blockade.

title: ROG Xbox Ally X gaming handheld to get Auto Super Resolution boost in April Microsoft touts 30% performance boost thanks to AI-powered image upscaling
summary: The ROG Xbox Ally X gaming handheld will get Auto Super Resolution, enabling AI-powered image upscaling that could deliver a 30% performance boost. This OS-level feature is currently available on Windows 11 and is expected to improve frame-rates. It is set to be released in April.
insight: AI upscaling could significantly enhance handheld gaming performance

title: Creality Sermoon P1 3D Scanner review: 3D scanning on the go
summary: The Creality Sermoon P1 is a portable 3D scanner that excels in speed and on-device editing capabilities. It features multiple scanning modes, including NIR mode without markers, and can handle challenging tasks like scanning deep holes. However, the color texture results were disappointing.
insight: Advanced 3D scanning on the go with seamless editing capabilities.

title: Iran conflict delays Meta s 2Africa undersea cable project cable layer declares force majeure, says it can no longer safely operate in the Persian Gulf
summary: Meta delays Africa2 network work due to Alcatel Submarine Networks' force majeure declaration, citing Iran conflict risks.
insight: Global conflicts can severely disrupt critical infrastructure projects worldwide instantly.

title: 11-month old Russian outfit claims it has developed 16-core and 32-core chips, flaunts Cyrillic-badged processors chips appear to be sanctions-swerving rebadged Chinese Loongson processors
summary: Russia's Tramplin Electronics claims to have developed 16-core and 32-core chips, but appear to be rebadged Chinese Loongson processors with Cyrillic labels.
insight: Sanctions evasion through processor rebadging and localization attempts

title: How to protect yourself from bad external SSDs during the PC hardware apocalypse newer drives will definitely cost more, and some may offer up shockingly poor performance
summary: Buying an external SSD soon may be wise due to potential shortages and increased costs as AI demand rises. Opt for drives released before the surge in AI-related purchases for better performance. This will help ensure you get a reliable and efficient external SSD.
insight: Buy older external SSDs before AI demand drives up costs.

title: 2026 PC shipment forecast slashed amid memory shortages IDC says total PC market value to nonetheless increase to $274 billion due to ongoing price hikes
summary: IDC forecasts an 11.3% drop in PC shipments in 2026, but a 1.6% increase in market value due to higher prices.
insight: Price hikes offset shipment decline in the PC market.

title: Silicon Power US RMA policy now hedges against AI-driven RAM and SSD shortages company says it will refund the original purchase price 'if there is a shortage of replacement products'
summary: Silicon Power US offers refunds in case of RAM/SSD shortages, hedging against supply chain issues.
insight: Companies adapt to mitigate AI-driven RAM/SSD shortages and supply chain risks

title: China bans OpenClaw from government computers and issues security guidelines amid adoption frenzy nation scrambles to rein in popular AI agent
summary: China's central government is restricting OpenClaw on government computers due to security concerns. This move aims to control the spread of the popular AI agent within government agencies. Multiple government bodies are involved in the effort to rein in OpenClaw.
insight: China is scrambling to regulate AI adoption amid security risks.

title: Microsoft debuts DirectStorage 1.4 at GDC 2026, with Zstandard compression and GACL update promises developers improved compression ratios, faster loading, and more
summary: Microsoft has released DirectStorage 1.4 at GDC 2026, introducing Zstandard compression and CreatorID for improved GPU scheduling. This update is expected to enhance compression ratios, loading speeds, and overall game performance. Developers stand to benefit from these improvements in game development.
insight: Faster loading and compression ratios boost game performance and experience.

title: Zombie ZIP vulnerability lets compressed malware leisurely stroll past 95% of antivirus apps security suites are blissfully unaware of security issue
summary: A 'Zombie ZIP' vulnerability allows malware to bypass 95% of antivirus software, putting users at risk. This security flaw is largely unknown to many antivirus suites, making it a significant concern. The vulnerability exploits a compression technique to evade detection.
insight: 95% of antivirus suites are unaware of the Zombie ZIP security issue.

title: The FBI is looking for victimized Steam users who downloaded games with hidden malware Investigation underway into multiple infected titles from 2024 to 2026
summary: The FBI is investigating malware-infected Steam games from 2024 to 2026, seeking victim information.
insight: Potential financial and identity theft risks for compromised Steam users.

title: RAM kits are now sold with one fake RAM stick alongside a real one to create a performance illusion 1+1 'value pack' offers desperate psychological relief as the memory shortage worsens
summary: V-Color introduces 1+1 DDR5 memory kits with a real and filler module for AMD systems. The kits promise performance boosts, but deliver a mixed result due to the inclusion of a fake memory stick. This tactic aims to alleviate memory shortages through perceived value.
insight: Manufacturer's attempt to create an illusion of better performance

title: Esparagus Audio Brick ESP32-based DIN-rail 65W Hi-Fi amplifier supports Home Assistant and Squeezelite (Crowdfunding)
summary: The Esparagus Audio Brick is a compact ESP32-based amplifier with Home Assistant support, ideal for building whole-home audio setups, retrofitting vintage speakers, or powering custom speaker configurations.
insight: Enhances home audio with a compact, ESP32-powered Hi-Fi Class-D amplifier solution.

title: Rockchip RK3538 TV Box SoC and RK3572 mid-range HMI processor are coming soon
summary: Rockchip is preparing to launch the RK3538 and RK3572 mid-range SoCs. The RK3538 is designed for TV boxes with a quad-core Cortex-A55 processor. The RK3572 targets Human Machine Interface applications with a hexa-core Cortex-A73/A53 SoC.
insight: Rockchip expands its mid-range SoC offerings for various applications.

title: Q/A with Pankaj Kedia: Sensors Converge keynoter talks Edge AI
summary: Pankaj Kedia predicts a significant revenue boost with Edge AI compared to cloud AI, citing a 10-to-1 ratio.
insight: Edge AI can generate 10 times more revenue than cloud AI.

title: New "Zombie ZIP" attack can evade most antivirus scanners
summary: A new attack, 'Zombie ZIP', uses malformed archives to evade antivirus scanners, potentially creating a new delivery mechanism for attackers. The technique hides malware inside compressed archives, making it difficult to detect. Researchers are debating whether it's a vulnerability or not.
insight: Attackers can now easily evade antivirus scanners with 'Zombie ZIP' archives

title: iPhone Fold leaks reveal key specs and a surprisingly competitive price tag
summary: Apple's first foldable iPhone, the iPhone Fold, is expected to be priced around $2,000, matching Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold 7. This price point is lower than previously rumored, making the foldable iPhone more competitive in the market. Gurman's prediction suggests Apple is taking a bold approach to foldable technology.
insight: Foldable iPhones may gain mainstream appeal with competitive pricing strategy

title: Google's Genie 3 AI can generate playable worlds, but they still fall apart after a minute
summary: Google's Genie 3 AI can generate playable worlds but has limitations.
insight: AI-powered world generation lacks long-term stability and coherence.

title: Meta's massive undersea cable project delayed in Persian Gulf as Iran conflict escalates
summary: Meta's 2Africa undersea cable project faces a new delay in the Persian Gulf due to escalating tensions with Iran.
insight: Geopolitical instability threatens global internet infrastructure and connectivity.

title: Adobe CEO to step down after 18 years as investors question the company's next act
summary: Adobe CEO to step down after 18 years, sparking stock drop.
insight: Leadership change raises concerns about Adobe's future direction.

title: HP's ink-blocking firmware may violate new global sustainability rules
summary: HP's ink-blocking firmware may violate global sustainability rules due to conflicts with EPEAT 2.0. The International Imaging Technology Council has expressed concerns over the requirements. The issue affects cartridge remanufacturers and printer users.
insight: HP's firmware may hinder cartridge recycling and sustainability efforts

title: Europe's PEGI will raise age ratings for games with loot boxes and microtransactions
summary: PEGI will introduce stricter age ratings for games with loot boxes and microtransactions, starting June.
insight: Stricter age ratings aim to protect minors from addictive monetization models.

title: MacBook Neo teardown reveals Apple's most repair-friendly laptop in years
summary: The MacBook Neo features a repair-friendly design with a removable keyboard, unlike recent MacBook models. This design allows for keyboard problems to be easily fixed without major surgery. The separate keyboard part makes maintenance and repairs more accessible.
insight: Separate keyboard design simplifies repair and maintenance significantly.

title: Downloaded a malware-infected Steam game? The FBI wants to hear from you
summary: The FBI's Seattle branch is investigating malware-infected Steam game downloads. They want to identify potential victims who installed these games. The goal is to gather information and prevent further harm.
insight: Malware in Steam games compromises users' sensitive personal and financial data.

title: Firefox 115 ESR is keeping Windows 7 support alive a little longer
summary: Firefox 115 ESR extends support for Windows 7 by six more months, allowing users of the outdated operating system to continue using a modern browser. This update was made without official announcement or widespread reporting. A small number of users will benefit from the extended support.
insight: Outdated OS users get temporary reprieve with modern browser support

title: Honda scraps three US-built EVs only months ahead of launch, takes $15.7 billion hit
summary: Honda has scrapped its three US-built EVs, incurring a $15.7 billion loss. The planned 0 Saloon and 0 SUV were initially set to launch in 2026. The decision indicates significant challenges in the US EV market.
insight: Failed US EV plans signal costly market misjudgment for Honda

title: Google Maps gets conversational with Gemini-powered "Ask Maps"
summary: Google Maps has introduced 'Ask Maps' powered by Gemini, enabling users to ask complex location-based questions.
insight: Simplifying location-based queries through conversational AI integration.
